Title: Putative rhamnogalacturonan-II glycosyltransferase identified through callus gene editing which bypasses embryo lethality

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: GT29 glycosyltransferase; ST-like glycosyltransferase; modified GT-A fold; N-terminal transmembrane domain
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for RG-II sidechain C formation; essential for normal RG-II borate cross-linking
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Callus culture in Arabidopsis root-derived tissue; no special environmental treatment central to interpretation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 knockout of AtRCKT1 in Arabidopsis callus caused RG-II to lose detectable Kdo. 2. Loss of RCKT1 strongly reduced RG-II dimerization/cross-linking, shifting RG-II toward monomer. 3. The data support RCKT1 as the RG-II CMP-β-Kdo transferase required to initiate sidechain C formation.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Developmental RNA-seq coexpression analysis using 113 public Arabidopsis datasets from ThaleMine; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: RG-II borate cross-linking is required for normal plant growth and development and proper primary cell wall function
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in callus; genotyping by Sanger sequencing/DECODR; RG-II isolation; SEC analysis of RG-II monomer/dimer; 1H-NMR; 2D TOCSY NMR; GC-EI-MS glycosyl residue analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Identified as highly coexpressed with primary cell wall- and GIPC-related genes across 113 developmental RNA-seq datasets
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Kdo-containing RG-II sidechain C (α-L-Rhap-(1→5)-α-D-Kdop disaccharide linkage on RG-II)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers Kdo from CMP-β-Kdo to the RG-II backbone/acceptor to form the α-2,3-linked Kdo that initiates RG-II sidechain C formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly coexpressed with primary cell wall- and GIPC-related genes across developmental datasets
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Committed/initiating step for RG-II sidechain C formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: CMP-β-Kdo and RG-II acceptor glycoform lacking sidechain C
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (Cas9 and DsRed in transformation vector); Arabidopsis U3 and U6 promoters (sgRNAs)
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms structural element required for target RG-II glycoform and enables borate-mediated dimerization
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Loss of RCKT1 abolishes detectable Kdo incorporation into RG-II and markedly impairs RG-II dimerization/cross-linking
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Homozygous mutant plants do not survive; gene appears essential for normal development
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Golgi (predicted/selected by SUBA); membrane-associated with N-terminal transmembrane domain
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also known as MGP2. AlphaFold/modeling supported a putative CMP-β-Kdo donor-binding site, but direct in vitro enzymatic activity was not yet demonstrated due to donor instability and unknown acceptor requirements.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering or dissecting cell-wall pectin architecture, but severe lethality requires tissue-specific, inducible, or partial editing strategies rather than constitutive whole-plant knockout

Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Callus culture in Arabidopsis root-derived tissue
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CRISPR/Cas9 callus editing generated homozygous and biallelic gmt1 mutants. 2. Mutant calli reproduced the known gmt1 GIPC chemotype with reduced mannosylation. 3. This validated the callus editing platform for studying essential glycosyltransferases.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Developmental RNA-seq coexpression analysis using 113 public Arabidopsis datasets from ThaleMine; no accession reported
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: GIPC headgroup glycosylation contributes to membrane sphingolipid structure and cell-cell adhesion-associated biology
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in callus; genotyping by Sanger sequencing/DECODR; GIPC TLC profiling; HPAEC-PAD monosaccharide analysis
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Used as query gene in coexpression analysis for cell-cell adhesion-related GTs
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers mannose onto GIPC sugar headgroups
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: CaMV 35S promoter (Cas9 and DsRed in transformation vector); Arabidopsis U3 and U6 promoters (sgRNAs)
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: gmt1 mutant callus shows strongly reduced GIPC mannose content and altered TLC mobility consistent with loss of mannosylation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Disrupting GMT1 causes severe plant growth defects and reduced cell-cell adhesion in prior studies
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included mainly as a validation target for the callus CRISPR strategy rather than the main discovery focus of the paper.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a benchmark locus for callus-based editing of essential glycosyltransferases; direct nutritional breeding value is limited
